etymology.description: 聲(声) (shēng, 'stone chime') + 殳(殳) (shū, 'hand holding a hammer or a mallet') = a stone chime being struck by a mallet
etymology.interpretation: The character 殸(qīng, 'sound of metal or stone') represents the sound produced by striking a stone chime with a mallet. It visually conveys the action of producing sound and is therefore an ideogrammic compound.

parts.english: hand holding a hammer or a mallet
